idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
Initial WMMA kernel with BM=64,BN=64,BK=16 - baseline for optimization
Larger tiles BM=128,BN=128,BK=32 with 2x2 fragments - warp mapping issue caused slowdown
PTX mma.sync with ldmatrix - incorrect register mapping, kernel runs but produces wrong accumulator access
WMMA with BM=BN=128, BK=32, 8 warps each computing 16x128 output with 2 K-steps per block
BM=256, BN=128, BK=32 with 8 warps each computing 32x128 - too many accumulators, low occupancy
Double buffering with synchronous loads - overhead exceeds latency hiding benefit
cp.async per-element copies are inefficient, pipeline likely broken
Vectorized float4 loads for global memory - 2x speedup from coalesced 128-bit accesses
Swizzled shared memory - overhead exceeds bank conflict savings
512 threads with incorrect warp-tile mapping caused verification failure
BK=64 with BM=BN=64 - low occupancy from 4 warps hurts more than arithmetic intensity gain
BK=64 - marginal difference from BK=32, no improvement
Split-K with incorrect fragment store pattern - verification failed
__ldg intrinsics - no improvement on Hopper unified L1 cache
Removed second __syncthreads() in K-loop - 3% speedup from reduced sync overhead
2x8 WMMA tiles with 4 warps - low occupancy and register pressure hurt performance
BN=64 broke load pattern, reported inf TFLOPS
--maxrregcount=128 - no improvement, slight regression
Persistent kernel with 132 blocks - insufficient parallelism for large grid
Tile swizzling - overhead exceeds L2 locality benefit
Double-buffered software pipelining - overhead exceeds benefit, increased SMEM pressure
2x4 warp tile (8 warps in 4x2 layout) - 7% speedup from better A-fragment reuse
4x4 warp tiles with 4 warps - register pressure kills occupancy
BM=256 with 16 warps (8x2) - 7.5% speedup from better A-matrix reuse
BM=256 BN=256 with 32 warps - load pattern mapping failed
BK=64 with BM=128 - had to reduce BM for SMEM, lost A-reuse benefit
Dynamic shared memory - high latency penalty, lower occupancy
BM=384 - verification failed, 16384 not divisible by 384
BM=512 with BK=16 - load pattern issue with 1024 threads
Padding +4 causes inf TFLOPS - likely memory alignment issue
4x2 warp tiles (4Mx2N warps) - 2% slower than 2x4 layout
3x4 warp tiles - register pressure kills performance
Explicit unroll hint - no change, compiler already optimizes
Restructured loop order - interleaving hurts more than helps
BM=256 BN=256 BK=16 with 32 warps - B load pattern issue
1x4 warp tiles with 32 warps - warp mapping error
launch_bounds(512,2) causes register spilling and incorrect results
remove-bounds-checks
notes: Removing bounds checks increased register usage from 124 to 136. With 512 threads, this exceeds 65536 registers/SM limit causing cudaErrorLaunchOutOfResources. Adding launch_bounds to constrain registers caused verification failure due to spilling.

idea: 4x2-warp-tiles
notes: Changed warp tiles from 2x4 to 4x2 (64x32 output per warp). Verification passes but severe performance regression from 106 to 24 TFLOPS. The 4x2 layout has worse memory access pattern for B matrix.

idea: BM128-BN256-transpose-tiles
notes: Swapped tile dimensions: BM=128, BN=256 (was 256x128). Warp layout changed to 4x4 with 2x4 tiles. Better B-matrix locality. NEW BEST: 109.4 TFLOPS (26.3% of cuBLAS), up from 106.
BN=384 doesn't divide 16384 evenly (42.67), causing incomplete coverage. Also 24 warps may have load pattern issues.

idea: BM64-BN256-small-block
notes: Smaller BM=64 tile reduces arithmetic intensity. 88 TFLOPS is worse than current best 109.

idea: BM128-BN512-large-block
notes: 32 warps (1024 threads) with 119 registers = 121856 registers, exceeds 65536 per SM. Register overflow.

idea: double-buffering-BM64-BN256
notes: Double buffering with BM=64 (to fit 2x smem). Pipelining helps latency but smaller tile reduces arithmetic intensity. 83 TFLOPS vs 109 TFLOPS best.

idea: BM192-BN256-3x4tiles
notes: BM=192 doesn't divide M=16384 evenly (85.33 blocks), causing incomplete coverage.

idea: BM128-BN128-square-tile
notes: Smaller square tile reduces arithmetic intensity. 98 TFLOPS vs 109 TFLOPS best.

idea: 2x2-warp-tiles-32warps
notes: 72 regs x 1024 threads = 73728 > 65536 register limit

idea: tile-swizzling-L2
notes: Tile swizzling for L2 cache locality. 109.3 TFLOPS same as iter040 (109.4). No improvement for large problem size.

idea: BM64-BN512
notes: BM=64 reduces A-matrix reuse too much. 82 TFLOPS vs 109 best.

idea: ldcg-cache-hint
notes: __ldcg cache hint for global loads. 107 TFLOPS slightly worse than 109 best.

idea: BK16-more-K-iterations
notes: BK=16 doubles K-loop iterations. 89 TFLOPS vs 109 best.

idea: interleave-load-compute
notes: Interleave fragment loads with mma. 108 TFLOPS vs 109 best.

idea: padding-16
notes: Increased padding from +8 to +16. 91 TFLOPS worse due to memory overhead.

idea: smem-align-256
notes: Added 256-byte alignment to shared memory. 108 TFLOPS same as 109 best.

idea: mma-loop-n-first
notes: Reversed mma loop order (N before M). 108 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: BM256-BN64
notes: BM=256 BN=64 - small BN reduces B-matrix reuse. 79 TFLOPS worse than 109 best.

idea: cooperative-groups-sync
notes: Used cg::thread_block.sync() instead of __syncthreads(). 109 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: disable-k-unroll
notes: pragma unroll 1 on K loop. 108 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: 1x2-warp-tiles-BM64-BN128
notes: Smaller 1x2 warp tiles. 79 TFLOPS worse than best.

idea: 4x2-warp-tiles-8warps
notes: 4x2 warp tiles with 8 warps - warp mapping broken

idea: threadfence-block
notes: Added __threadfence_block before syncthreads. 107 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: use-fast-math
notes: Compiled with --use_fast_math. 108 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: maxrregcount-64
notes: Register limit 64 caused spilling and verification failure.

idea: BK64-BM64
notes: BK=64 with BM=64 - load pattern not updated for new dimensions

idea: O2-optimization
notes: Compiled with -O2 instead of -O3. 108 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: cache-all-loads
notes: Compiled with -Xptxas -dlcm=ca. 108 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: cache-global-only
notes: Compiled with -Xptxas -dlcm=cg. 108 TFLOPS same as best.

idea: Double buffering with BM=64 BN=256 - arithmetic intensity too low
cp.async for async global-to-shared memory transfers - 35% improvement
cp.async + double buffer BK=16 - reduced arithmetic intensity hurts more than pipelining helps
cp.async BM=128 BN=256 - BM=256 BN=128 config is better
BK=64 exceeds 48KB shared memory limit
3x3 warp tiles with BM=BN=192 - slight regression
Removing bounds checks caused verification failure
4x4 warp layout with 1x1 tiles failed verification - warp mapping issue
Padding +4 broke timing - likely alignment issue with WMMA stride
Padding +16 wastes smem bandwidth - regression from +8
8 warps with 4x4 tiles - register pressure hurts more than reduced warp overhead helps
Smaller tiles BM=128 BN=128 BK=16 for higher occupancy - less compute per memory load hurts
2-stage pipeline with BK=16 - verification failed due to indexing bug
256x256 tile with BK=16 - verification failed, warp indexing bug
XOR swizzle breaks WMMA load layout - inf TFLOPS timing error
maxrregcount=128 capped registers but didnt improve - register spilling hurts
BK=48 BM=192 - reduced parallelism hurts more than larger K helps
Removing syncthreads broke correctness - need sync between K iterations
BM=128 BK=48 - load pattern bug with non-divisible thread distribution
BM=128 BK=64 - larger K tile increases compute density, 4 WMMA_K ops per iteration
BK=96 exceeds 48KB smem limit
BK=80 load pattern indexing bug - non-divisible row distribution
BM=64 BK=64 - too few warps per block reduces compute efficiency
Warp tile 1x8 - worse than 2x4, more B-matrix loads per warp
Warp tile 4x2 - better A-matrix reuse per warp, 0.3 TFLOPS improvement
Warp tile 3x3 with only 4 warps - insufficient parallelism hurts
Reduced register pressure with maxrregcount=64, smaller BM=64 - occupancy improved but compute/memory throughput dropped
3-stage double-buffered pipeline - verification failed due to incorrect pipeline state management
BM=256 BN=64 for improved A matrix reuse - worse due to reduced parallelism in N dimension
BK=128 with dynamic shared memory - more compute per K iteration but higher smem pressure
WGMMA requires CUTLASS/CuTe headers and complex PTX - pivoting to choreo DSL for WGMMA access
Vectorized stores via shared memory staging - smem overflow (128KB needed)
BN=192 tile - verification failed due to load pattern bug
